mechanism_narrative: Sustained real GDP growth as measured by GDPC1 indicates expansion in aggregate output and corporate earnings, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year rank IC between GDPC1 growth and subsequent 60-day returns is below 0.05, or the signal's Sharpe ratio is negative over any 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate signals labor market tightening, which boosts consumer income and spending, leading to higher corporate profits and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE decreases by more than 0.2 percentage points over a 3-month period
failure_condition: Average 20-day forward return after trigger activation is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.10) or hit rate is below 55% over at least 10 years of data.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is very low and industrial production is rising, accommodative monetary policy amplifies real economic momentum, producing a strong positive impulse for equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term of FEDFUNDS and INDPRO in a predictive regression has p-value > 0.05, or the rank IC of the combined signal is below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a low interest rate regime (FEDFUNDS < 1%), strong retail sales growth indicates robust consumption that is heavily discounted at low rates, directly boosting equity valuations over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The rank IC of RSAFS in low-rate periods is not significantly higher than in high-rate periods (two-sample t-test p < 0.05), or the overall rank IC is non-positive over a full market cycle.

mechanism_narrative: Positive monthly changes in industrial production signal expanding manufacturing output and supply chain activity, which boosts short-term market sentiment and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The 5-day forward return after positive INDPRO releases is not positive more than 55% of the time, or the average excess return is not statistically different from zero (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year CPI inflation rises above a moderate threshold, it signals that demand is recovering and deflation risks have receded, which supports risk appetite and corporate pricing power, leading to positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: After trigger activation, the average 60-day forward return is negative or not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) over a 20-year sample, or the rank IC is below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with a falling unemployment rate jointly signal a self-reinforcing expansion in housing demand and labor income, which strongly predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction between HOUST and UNRATE in predicting 20-day returns is not significantly positive (regression coefficient p > 0.05) over any 5-year window, or the combined signal's rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Large monthly increases in nonfarm payrolls indicate strong labor demand and income growth, providing an immediate boost to consumer confidence and equity prices over the following trading week.
failure_condition: After trigger, the 5-day forward return is positive less than 55% of the time, or the average return is not significantly different from zero (t-test p > 0.10) over any 15-year backtest.
